brand.name: Varsity Tutors
brand.positioning: Lead with outcomes, not features. Confident but not arrogant. Expert but not elitist. Meet people where they are.

sat_tutoring.description: SAT tutoring — monthly membership with expert 1:1 sessions, diagnostics, study plan, practice tests, and progress tracking

verified_claims.claim: 1-on-1 expert SAT tutoring with vetted tutors
Online format mirrors the real Digital SAT interface
Students raise scores 10X more than self-study
Students raise scores 2.6X more than group classes, local tutors, and brand-name programs
~100 points per month at 2 sessions/week + 20min/day practice
Over 60% of SAT math can be solved with built-in calculator and formulas
6 full-length Digital SAT practice tests included
Weekly progress reports with projected score
Perfect tutor match guarantee — rematch immediately if not right fit
College essay support included after SAT

pain_points.point: Comparing prep options (Princeton Review, Khan Academy, Chegg, Kaplan)
Wanting the best for their child
GPA/SAT score mismatch — smart kid, disappointing test score

emotional_drivers.driver: Wanting the best for their child
Parental exhaustion — tired of being homework police

athlete_recruit.description: Mother of 11th-grade student athlete. SAT is a gating factor for scholarships, NCAA/NAIA eligibility, or coach support.
athlete_recruit.psychology: Fear of missed window + urgency + identity (athletic dream). Hard work on the field should not be undermined by a test score.
athlete_recruit.trigger: Recruiting window open, SAT score needed for eligibility or scholarship. Specific math gaps (Algebra 2, word problems).
athlete_recruit.preferred_cta: Talk to an SAT specialist today. We'll call you in 60 seconds.

suburban_optimizer.description: Upper-middle-class suburban mother, 11th grader with 3.8+ GPA but mid-1200s SAT. Dual-income household in affluent district.
suburban_optimizer.psychology: Control, optimization, fairness. Doesn't want child leaving points on the table vs. peers who prepared more.
suburban_optimizer.trigger: PSAT or first SAT score in mid-1200s doesn't match classroom performance. 'She wants a 1450.'
key_needs: Clear plan (diagnostic, gap analysis, 30-40 hours over 8-10 weeks)
suburban_optimizer.preferred_cta: See what score range is realistic in 8–10 weeks.

immigrant_navigator.description: First/second-gen immigrant family (South Asian, Middle Eastern, Latin American). Mother calling 60%, student as proxy 40%.
immigrant_navigator.psychology: Overwhelm + unfamiliar system + fear of making wrong choice. Education as primary path to upward mobility.
immigrant_navigator.trigger: Navigating test prep for first time, or returning after disappointing tutoring. Need education about SAT itself before evaluating providers.
immigrant_navigator.preferred_cta: Talk to an SAT specialist who will walk you through it step by step.

cultural_investor.description: Dual-income STEM professionals with high father involvement. Education spending is non-negotiable. Student in accelerated track.
cultural_investor.psychology: Process-oriented, already investing in multiple resources. Values mastery over quick performance gains.
cultural_investor.trigger: Grade-related concern (AP Physics/Calculus drop), frustration that prior tutoring didn't improve performance.
key_needs: Consolidate scattered resources into one system
cultural_investor.preferred_cta: See how we replace five tools with one system that actually raises scores.

system_optimizer.description: Corporate father (75%+ calling), tech-centric regions. Approaches tutoring as procurement decision, not emotional one.
system_optimizer.psychology: Efficiency, process optimization, measurable outcomes. Wants scope, method, timeline, result — not marketing.
system_optimizer.trigger: Approaching SAT date, expiring employer benefit (Bright Horizons), frustration with cumbersome setup.

neurodivergent_advocate.description: Mother of 9th-12th grader with ADHD, autism, dyslexia, dyscalculia, or processing disorder. Deep knowledge of child's diagnosis.
neurodivergent_advocate.psychology: Protective + 'my child learns differently' + fear of being misunderstood. Values right fit over lowest price.
neurodivergent_advocate.trigger: Approaching test date + realization that standard prep won't work. Prior tutoring failed because instructor couldn't adapt.
key_needs: Honesty about tutor experience with similar students
Follow-up in writing with tutor profiles and accommodation plan
neurodivergent_advocate.preferred_cta: Tell us about your child. We'll match them with a tutor who understands how they learn.

burned_returner.description: Mother of 11th/12th grader after prior negative tutoring experience (with VT or competitor). Defining characteristic is disappointment.
burned_returner.psychology: Trust deficit + urgency + conditional willingness. Core fear: being burned again.
burned_returner.trigger: New academic crisis forcing return despite past disappointment. May reference specific tutor by name or describe grades declining during prior tutoring.
key_needs: Acknowledgment and ownership of past failure
Concrete explanation of what will be different
burned_returner.preferred_cta: Tell us what went wrong. We'll show you what's different.

dos.rule: Use 'your child' — parents think of them as their children, not students
Use avg or typical score gains/improvements with conditions
100pts/month if attending 2x/week 1:1 sessions, 1 weekly practice test, 20min/day practice
Mention that every X points on SAT is worth Y dollars in scholarship/merit aid
Compare to competitors with real data: self-study, learning centers, local tutors, group courses
Compare results: 10X the score improvement of self-study, 2.6X of in-person/group/courses
Compare value: Princeton Review/Kaplan $199-$252/hr for 1:1, VT as low as $349/mth
Digital SAT advantage: taken on laptop, 60%+ of math solved with built-in tools, students trained on paper run out of time
Use calendar urgency: test dates, application deadlines, weeks remaining, sessions possible
Use specific mechanisms: describe HOW the digital SAT is different, HOW tutoring works
Make claims credible with conditions: '16 sessions → 200 points', 'if score is 1100-1300'

donts.rule: Never use vague score claims without specificity
Never claim to help them actually get a scholarship
Never market as 'online tutoring' — parents dismiss before hearing the case
Never use fake urgency: 'spots filling fast', 'limited enrollment', 'secure their spot', 'don't miss out'
Never use corporate/marketing language: 'unlock potential', 'maximize score', 'tailored support', 'custom strategies', 'growth areas', 'dream college within reach'

self_paced_courses.vt_advantage: No one identifying what your child specifically struggles with

group_courses.format: Group Zoom class, 1:1 is extra charge
group_courses.vt_advantage: 2.6X the score improvement, 1:1 included not add-on

local_tutors.format: 1 person, no platform, no diagnostics, no practice tests

varsity_tutors.format: 1:1 expert + full platform + study plan + diagnostics + practice tests

offer.model: Monthly SAT tutoring membership — not a course, not a group class, not a self-study app

includes: 2 hour-long 1:1 sessions per week with their tutor
1 full-length Digital SAT practice test per week
Study plan built specifically for them with daily assigned lessons and practice problems

whats_included: Training on Real Digital SAT Interface
offer.score_improvement: ~100 points per month at 2 sessions/week + 20min/day practice
offer.super_score: Students who score 1400+ typically take the SAT 3 times on average. Membership continues with same tutor and progress.
offer.cancellation: Cancel anytime. No long-term commitments, no penalties.

proof_points.claim: Students raise scores 10X more than self-study
Students raise scores 2.6X more than group classes, local tutors, and brand-name programs
~100 points per month at 2 sessions/week + 20min/day practice
Over 60% of SAT math can be solved with built-in calculator and formulas
Students who score 1400+ typically take the SAT 3 times

persona_specific.athlete_recruit: Talk to an SAT specialist today. We'll call you in 60 seconds.
persona_specific.suburban_optimizer: See what score range is realistic in 8–10 weeks.
persona_specific.immigrant_navigator: Talk to an SAT specialist who will walk you through it step by step.
persona_specific.cultural_investor: See how we replace five tools with one system that actually raises scores.
persona_specific.neurodivergent_advocate: Tell us about your child. We'll match them with a tutor who understands how they learn.
persona_specific.burned_returner: Tell us what went wrong. We'll show you what's different.
persona_specific.scholarship: See how many scholarship dollars your score could unlock.
persona_specific.test_anxiety: See how we help students walk into the SAT feeling ready, not scared.
persona_specific.accountability: Get a tutor who keeps your child accountable — so you don't have to.
persona_specific.school_failed: See what real SAT tutoring looks like.
persona_specific.sibling: Let's build the plan that works for this child.

never_claim: Fear-based language implying the child is deficient
Fake urgency ('spots filling fast', 'limited enrollment', 'don't miss out')
Claiming to help get scholarships directly
always_include: Factual basis for any claims (sourced from this knowledge base)
Appropriate CTA for the funnel stage and persona
On-brand tone matching the target audience/persona profile
Specific mechanisms (how tutoring works, how digital SAT is different)

gap_report.concept: The Gap Report — ad looks like an internal performance dashboard or consulting deliverable, not a tutoring ad
gap_report.visual_direction: White/light background, clean sans-serif, no stock photos, no lifestyle imagery. Data only. McKinsey one-pager aesthetic.
gap_report.palette: White, black, one accent (dark navy or muted green 'on track' indicator)
gap_report.headline_example: Gap: 190 points. Timeline: 10 weeks. Result: Closed.
gap_report.layout: Header (small logo) → Headline → INPUT/OUTPUT spec table → Bottom strip CTA
gap_report.cta: Start with a free diagnostic. Know the gap before you spend a dollar.
gap_report.variant_headline: Princeton Review: $252/hr for group instruction. Varsity Tutors: 1:1 sessions, diagnostic-driven plan, 210-point avg gain. Do the math.

meta_ad_structure.format: Hook (1 sentence) → Short pattern interrupt explanation (2–3 lines max) → Micro-commitment CTA

example.body: The SAT isn't a classroom test. Most strong students are 3–4 targeted fixes away from a 1400+. We diagnose exactly where points are hiding.
example.cta: See what score is realistic in 8–10 weeks.
